refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms end-to-end to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR specifies teleoperation latency compatibility with safe remote operation. Refining it to a measurable latency threshold (p95 ≤ 150 ms) aligns with acceptance criteria and safety criticality.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end communication latency under agreed network conditions using telemetry data during teleoperation sessions.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the teleoperation data path.
FR2: Ensure reliable and timely data transmission over the network link.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry feedback to the operator.

candidate_dps: DP1: Optimize data encoding and compression algorithms to reduce payload size.
DP2: Use dedicated low-latency network protocols and QoS prioritization.
DP3: Implement edge processing to minimize data transmission distance.
DP4: Design telemetry data aggregation and buffering strategies.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP2: Use dedicated low-latency network protocols and QoS prioritization.

potential_sources: Coupling between telemetry data generation and network protocol implementation.
Dependency on network link quality and configuration.
coupling_check.notes: DP2 logically isolates latency reduction to network protocol and QoS, minimizing cross-domain coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network infrastructure supports QoS and low-latency protocols.
Risk of network congestion or failure impacting latency despite protocol optimization.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating edge processing (DP3) if network conditions degrade or latency targets are not met.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Responsible for implementing network protocols, managing telemetry data transmission, and ensuring latency targets.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Telemetry data sent from Vehicle to Teleoperation module.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Network communication channel used for teleoperation data exchange.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Commands received from NGHE Solution or Site Manager for teleoperation control.

module_containment_scores.notes: High-level coordination but not focused on latency optimization.
Generates telemetry but does not control network protocols.
Primary module responsible for latency-sensitive network protocol implementation.
Focuses on safety alerts, not communication latency.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Latency-critical communication functions are best contained within the Teleoperation module to minimize diffusion and maximize control.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle module telemetry data quality and timing.
Network link variability outside module control may impact latency.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Monitor interface timing and consider tighter integration with Vehicle telemetry if latency targets are not met.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP (low-latency network protocols and QoS) directly addresses the refined FR latency requirement with clear verification paths.

diffusion_risks: Telemetry data dependency on Vehicle module may introduce latency variability.
Network link external factors could diffuse control over latency.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module has high containment score and ownership suitability for DP implementation.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be measured end-to-end across Teleoperation and Vehicle modules using Telemetry and Network Link interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear ownership of latency-critical functions in Teleoperation module with defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Refine telemetry data timing requirements with Vehicle module.
Establish monitoring of network link quality and latency impact.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition sufficiently refines the FR, selects a viable DP, and assigns a clear IM boundary with manageable risks and verification feasibility.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the teleoperation data path.
FR2: Ensure reliable and timely data transmission over the network link.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry feedback to the operator.

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Network infrastructure supports QoS and low-latency protocols.
Telemetry data from Vehicle is timely and accurate.
open_risks: Network congestion or failure impacting latency.
evidence_needed: Latency measurements under operational network conditions.
Telemetry data timing and quality analysis.
